Treats.label: Treats
Treats.description: A drug, procedure, or intervention is used to treat or manage a disease or condition. Example: 'Aspirin treats myocardial infarction.'

Causes.label: Causes
Causes.description: An entity (e.g., gene, chemical, behavior) leads to a disease or condition. Example: 'BRCA1 mutation causes breast cancer.'

Associated-With.label: Associated-With
Associated-With.description: A general or correlative association between entities. Example: 'Obesity is associated with diabetes.'

Expressed-In.label: Expressed-In
Expressed-In.description: Describes the tissue or cell type where a gene/protein is expressed. Example: 'ACE2 is expressed in lung epithelial cells.'

Located-In.label: Located-In
Located-In.description: Spatial containment or localization of an entity. Example: 'Tumor is located in the pancreas.'

Part-Of.label: Part-Of
Part-Of.description: One entity is a constituent or component of another. Example: 'Alveoli are part of the lungs.'

Subtype-Of.label: Subtype-Of
Subtype-Of.description: A more specific subtype of a broader category. Example: 'Glioblastoma is a subtype of brain tumor.'

Interacts-With.label: Interacts-With
Interacts-With.description: Biochemical or pharmacological interaction between entities. Example: 'Warfarin interacts with Vitamin K.'

Inhibits.label: Inhibits
Inhibits.description: Suppressive relationship where one entity reduces the activity of another. Example: 'Aspirin inhibits COX-1 enzyme.'

Activates.label: Activates
Activates.description: An entity increases the expression or activity of another. Example: 'IL-6 activates STAT3 signaling.'

Diagnoses.label: Diagnoses
Diagnoses.description: A diagnostic tool or marker identifies a disease. Example: 'MRI diagnoses brain tumor.'

Has-Symptom.label: Has-Symptom
Has-Symptom.description: A disease presents a clinical symptom. Example: 'COVID-19 has symptom fever.'

Used-For.label: Used-For
Used-For.description: An entity serves a general medical or diagnostic purpose. Example: 'CT scan is used for tumor detection.'

Contraindicated-For.label: Contraindicated-For
Contraindicated-For.description: An entity should not be used for a specific condition. Example: 'NSAIDs are contraindicated for peptic ulcer.'

Biomarker-Of.label: Biomarker-Of
Biomarker-Of.description: An entity serves as a biomarker for a disease or condition. Example: 'CA-125 is a biomarker of ovarian cancer.'

Agonism-or-Antagonism.label: Agonism-or-Antagonism
Agonism-or-Antagonism.description: A compound activates or blocks a biological target. Example: 'Tamoxifen antagonizes estrogen receptor.'

Binding.label: Binding
Binding.description: Describes direct binding between molecules. Example: 'Ligand binds to receptor.'

Affects-Expression.label: Affects-Expression
Affects-Expression.description: Regulation of gene expression by a drug or another gene. Example: 'TNF-alpha induces IL-6 expression.'

Metabolizes.label: Metabolizes
Metabolizes.description: A gene/protein metabolizes a drug or chemical. Example: 'CYP3A4 metabolizes warfarin.'

Transports.label: Transports
Transports.description: Transport of a substance by a protein or gene. Example: 'SLC6A4 transports serotonin.'

Enzyme-Activity.label: Enzyme-Activity
Enzyme-Activity.description: Describes modulation of enzymatic activity. Example: 'Fluoride inhibits enolase activity.'

Side-Effect.label: Side-Effect
Side-Effect.description: Indicates adverse effects of a drug or treatment. Example: 'Aspirin causes gastric bleeding.'

Contributes-To-Pathogenesis.label: Contributes-To-Pathogenesis
Contributes-To-Pathogenesis.description: The entity plays a mechanistic role in disease development. Example: 'Smoking contributes to lung cancer.'

Mutation-Affects-Disease.label: Mutation-Affects-Disease
Mutation-Affects-Disease.description: A gene variant contributes to disease risk. Example: 'BRCA1 mutation increases breast cancer risk.'

Gene-Regulates-Gene.label: Gene-Regulates-Gene
Gene-Regulates-Gene.description: Regulatory interaction between genes. Example: 'TP53 regulates BAX.'
